titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 14: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 14. (A.R. No. - of 2003.) OLIYAMAṄGALAM, ILUPPUR TALUK, PUDUKKOTTAI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a stone lying in the tank of the village.

This inscription is in verse composed in kaṭṭaḷaikalittuṟai metre. This record may be assigned to Parāntaka I not only on account of its paleography but also due to the occurrence of the name Vīrachōḷa, an epithet assumed by him.

It records that when the peerless Virachōḷa was ruling (a person called) Chōḷakulanārāyaṇa Mūvēndavēḷ, caused a sluice built to the tank at Ollaiyūr in the fourth regnal year of the king. He (the builder of the sluice) is eulogized in this record as generous, he wore a fragrant flower garland and (he was also called) Kēśavan who belonged to Iḍaikuḍi where Māyavaṉ ie God Vishṇu was pleased to reside and bless.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . naṭaiyā
r tanic ceṅkol vira
coḻaṟki yāṇṭu nālā
ṇṭil kuṭaiyār va
ṇkai Ollai Ūr ku
ḷattuk kumiḻi kuyiṟṟuvi
tton toṭaiyārala
ṅkaṟ coḻakula nārāya
ṇa mūventaveḷ Aṭai
yār ciyamiṭaikkuṭi mā
yava nilavuṅ kecava
ney .
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 14 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
